    Wentao

    Weekly Ofs 43

    April 20, 2025 8 minute read

    1. semiconductor feng shui 101: which VCSEL shape generates better random numbers
      • Lu2025: Ultrafast dynamics in tailored VCSEL cavity geometry toward high-efficiency physical random number generation
      • at “800 Gb/s with minimal post-processing”
      • everything goes in and out of the square hole
    2. Every time I have to drive for a while, I think about a car with tires made of solid kevlar so that it could go at mach 4.6 without the tires disintegrate.

    (ignoring all other more important aspects lol)

    • so ThrustSSC is using some aerospace-grade shit for its solid aluminum tire (it has a target speed of 850 mph, and managed to reach 760 mph and break the sound barrier)
    • Forgot to include that the second image is from wikipedia, the 3rd one is from Dunlop Aviation Supplies Wheels And Brakes For ThrustSSC
    1. the OG of kiki and bouba are actually takete and maluma (from 1929)
      • Köhler1929: Gestalt psychology it’s actually more than 100 years old!
      • found through The size and shape of sound: The role of articulation and acoustics in iconicity and crossmodal correspondences
      • this is gold I’m gonna start calling low pass filters the moe-type filters, and high pass filters the tsun-type filters

    2. wait why do Unaxis and Plasma-Therm have the same sets of GUI icons? looks around oh Plasma-Therm was sold to Unaxis and then a buyout

      • Shuttleline ICP/RIE etch tool intro Plasma-Therm Metal Etcher Plasma-Therm celebrates first year of independence and reports positive financial results for FY/2009 Meanwhile chatGPT:
    3. measuring location and radiation pattern of car noise sources with a microphone array
      • Dreier2025: Speed-dependent directivity patterns of road-traffic vehicles

    4. “an open software toolbox for signal acquisition, processing, and inspection in acoustics” Opens up the repo: MATLAB

      • Paper: AKtools–an open software toolbox for signal acquisition, processing, and inspection in acoustics code: f-brinkmann/AKtools
      • octave is great. I doubt it would work though, it’s probably using a bunch of toolboxes that need separate licensing
    5. laser beam oscillation vs weld quality, and the characterization setup
      • Börner2025: X-ray analysis of capillary and melt pool dynamics using laser beam oscillation welding for aluminum die-casts you know there are people out there with equipments and access that you could never affort, and just put the paper out for free.
    6. are there 4+ turbo pumps attached to this big chamber (main vacuum chamber of the Apollon laser facility, in Saclay, France)
      • Yao2025: Characterization and performance of the Apollon main short-pulse laser beam following its commissioning at 2 PW level More at APOLLON Laser Facility - Short focal area.
    7. this must feel so good for the chip

    (capillary underfill (CUF) during flip-chip encapsulation)

    • Nordson ASYMTEK: The NexJet System - Flip Chip Underfill Comprehensive Dispensing & Melt Creeping Simulation for IC Capillary Underfill Process
    • they have quite some impressive examples Optimizing High Precision Molding Process of Optical Components using Moldex3D CAE Simulation Analysis
    • like these?
    1. do journals no longer care about making a high res good cover anymore now?
      • Physical Review Letters Volume 134, Issue 14 The cover paper itself is poetic, tfw you understand every single word but also don’t understand a word
    2. removed the cover inside, now you can see more stuff (this is a 215M, very similar to 315M)
      • play with it yourself here (only has 0th order SH..): https://jwt625.github.io/splat/?url=https://huggingface.co/datasets/jwt625/splat/resolve/main/coherent_laser_v2_20070912_20250414.ply… Schematics from Sam’s Laser FAQ - Photos of Coherent Diode Pumped Solid State Lasers
      • very cool what is this using?
      • i wish im this rich
      • not really, it’s directly taken by an iphone camera would love to add a lens and get closer
    3. nice example showing why grid mesh could be dumb
      • Fei2025: Experimental and numerical investigation of the damage effects in an underwater confined box subjected to internal blast loading
      • interesting, haven’t heard about them before, nice name
      • it’s just quite some of the rest of the domain is meshed unnecessarily dense because it has to be dense around one point
    4. semiconductor feng shui 101, session 2: which electrode shape kills more spurious modes in a bulk acoustic wave resonator.
      • Berger2025: Fully dielectric acoustic Bragg mirror of a-SiOCN:H and a-SiC:H layers for bulk acoustic wave resonators
      • it’s the pentagonal one (peaks from spurious modes more smooth / lower Q)
      • it’s the pentagonal one (peaks from spurious modes more smooth / lower Q)
      • ha i used the same fbar image in my recent blog
      • the vcsel rng is for super fast rate
    5. high power VCSEL array for pumping solid state lasers, from Princeton Optronics
      • Xiong2012: High power VCSEL array pumped Q-switched Nd:YAG lasers
      • im now a fan of vcsel pumped solid state lasers, look how cute and scary they are Scaled-up 2D solid-state laser array
    6. If I win the lottery, there will be signs
      • Applied Materials - Endura® PVD
      • good for you im jealous
      • nice any examples?
      • added to cart
      • we’d need volume similar to mrbeast or top onlyfans earners
      • Applied Materials - Endura® PVD
      • they kind of are, for really good shit
    7. Watch the man himself walk you thru how to build a quantum supercomputer, and the scaling challenges and opportunities

    (look at that atomically sharp Al/Si interface)

    • How to Build a Quantum Supercomputer - Scaling Challenges and Opportunities (submitted by a friend that would like to remain anonymous)
    • folks at amat are real deal
    1. now this is real HDR photography

    (a “semiconductor membrane laser” made with a 600 nm film of quantum wells sandwiched between two diamond heat spreaders)

    • Kahle2016: Semiconductor membrane external-cavity surface-emitting laser (MECSEL)
    • they did not say anything looking at the picture it might, multiple scatterings off the setup is pretty clear
    1. “optical neural network,” they said, “it’s going to be the new paradigm after Moore’s Law is dead,” they said.

    Meanwhile, a 3-layer optical DNN:

    (this is a joke & not fair, similar things have been done on a chip, but still need similar control & stabilization etc)

    • Davis2022: RF-Photonic Deep Learning Processor with Shannon-Limited Data Movement This latency breakdown is cool
    • correction, it is only the “first photoelectric multiplication”, not the full 3-layer DNN. Still infinitely better than people who do not include pictures of the actual thing.
    • one day I shall sit down and read the original proposal in details. The core idea is using homodyne/heterodyne detection as the matrix multiplication step, and the optical fanout is relatively easy and energy efficient. I really like the idea. Hamerly2019: [Large-Scale Optical
    • one of the recent experiment implementation with VCSELs, and a company spun out of it
    • maybe it’s it takes time, we are still early but again I believe comm & sensing makes way more sense for light than compute
    1. Look at that subtle transparency. The tasteful thickness of it. Oh my God, it even has shadows… choking with anxiety
      • Huang2025: Logical multi-qubit entanglement with dual-rail superconducting qubits If you are interested in the flip-chip process: Satzinger2019: Simple non-galvanic flip-chip integration method for hybrid quantum systems
    2. first glance: ok cool another fiber array one second later: cultured meat? hollow fiber bioreactor??

    (scale bar is 2 cm)

    • Nie2025: Scalable tissue biofabrication via perfusable hollow fiber arrays for cultured meat applications
    • what gcamp laser paper, GCaMP?
    • lool the list of niche insults keep growing (my fav is still sound absorber)
    • or spider silk
    • next they’ll plug these fibers into us directly
    • they have a figure for the texture and taste that i do not understand except it’s better than the control
    1. nice rf/microwave connectors guide from mini-circuits
      • Care & Handling of RF/Microwave Connectors this tutorial from the signal path is also very informative: TSP #26 - Tutorial on Microwave and mm-Wave Components and Modules
    2. why would you do this (cryo-VCSEL) instead of sending light from room T to 4 K and use a modulator? even cots bulk modulators have comparable energy-per-bit, and fancy on-chip ones are orders of magnitude better.
      • I do like they have cooled down a cal kit. Wu2024: Cryo-VCSELs Operated at 2.8 K and 40 K With Record Bandwidth, Power, and Linearity for Optical Data Links in Quantum Computing
      • great question, in principle there is no need, you could do phase modulation, and there will be no power loss from the input photons. In parctice there will always be insertion loss etc, and it is indeed the dominating energy cost (at least in the case of quantum frequency
    3. A timeline of molecular and crystallographic visualizations
      • Horton2023: Crystal Toolkit: A Web App Framework to Improve Usability and Accessibility of Materials Science Research Algorithms

    4. “killed flash memory and DRAM in one shot” looks inside: mechanical exfoliated and dry-transferred graphene and WSe2

      • Xiang2025: Subnanosecond flash memory enabled by 2D-enhanced hot-carrier injection This is how mechanical exfoliation looks like: [2D Materials Enabled Next-Generation Integrated Optoelectronics: from Fabrication to
      • actually found one photo when I was doing it back in 2015
      • you are optimistic. Sentiment analysis says it’s 98.9% negative
      • nice high mobility kind of clean?
      • https://x.com/jwt0625/status/1913452119161724964… many aspects of the process is not scalable, such as this (and variants) is still the sota method to get high quality graphene they need, and you get unpreditable size & quantities of monolayer graphene at random locations on a chip. You can CVD it but
      • they probably need to figure out a solution for the birth rate problem first
    5. semiconductor feng shui 101, session 3: which aperture cruciform shape & orientation exorcise the wrong polarization faster.
      • Yuan2025: Enhanced polarization locking in VCSELs
      • they have a big table for optical injection locked lasers
      • if i could spot one thats good and fun
      • they have a big table for optical injection locked lasers
      • if i could spot one thats good and fun
    6. directly soldering components onto a chip looks so wrong..
      • Russo2025: Superconducting microwave oscillators as detectors for ESR spectroscopy
      • there are interposers and PCBs in between with well tested BE and packaging process, the above is directly onto the chip as a one-off lol (I don’t like it also because they are using superconductors on the chip for potential high quality factor/low noise, but then put all these
      • nothing really wrong with this as it’s research & non standard (their chip is not built to host components directly, e.g. UBM has a few layers for putting on solder, while they have YBCO + Au. This is fine for them but might see problem after things like temperature cycling 1000x
    7. you could listen to how air gets zapped by the pulses from a frequency comb, to detect the carrier-envelope phase (CEP).

    (the blurry image of the setup is all they got..)

    • Han2025: Hearing carrier-envelope offset frequency and phase in air with a microphone

    Updated: April 20, 2025
